qualifying_org_definition: Implicit federal standard (501(c)(3)/government/place of worship); RI DHS does not publish a narrower state-level definition; risnapet.org lists suggestions (United Way, ServeRI, faith-based orgs, libraries, animal rescues) as possible volunteer opportunities but these are illustrative, not a pre-approved registry
verification_instrument: DHS SNAP ABAWD-3 Combined Activity Reporting Form (org-signed participant log also accepted)
hours_formula_detail: RI DHS documents explicitly state ABAWDs must work and/or volunteer at least 80 hours a month; this matches the federal default under 7 CFR 273.24. No workfare formula used (RI FY26 State Plan confirms workfare component is not offered).
accepts_outofstate_detail: No RI DHS policy document — including the FY26 E&T State Plan, the ABAWD-3 form, the ABAWD fact sheet, or the ABAWD town-change letter — restricts certifying organizations to in-state nonprofits. The ABAWD-3 form asks only for the activity location and a supervisor signature, not the nonprofit's state of incorporation or registration. Under the federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying organization may verify hours. Absent an explicit in-state restriction, out-of-state 501(c)(3)s should be operationally accepted.
geographic_waivers: Partial waiver, expiration uncertain as of mid-2026. As of July 1, 2024, five towns were waived through June 30, 2025: Central Falls, Charlestown, New Shoreham (Block Island), Providence, and Woonsocket. The FY26 E&T State Plan (approved August 2025) projects 1,500 of 3,000 ABAWDs in waived areas, suggesting some waiver continued into FY2026. The prior (pre-July 2024) waiver covered 23 towns through June 30, 2024. No public document confirms the exact list or end date of waivers currently in effect in 2026; contact RI DHS for current status.
submission_deadline: ABAWD-3 form must be submitted at application, interim review, or recertification. If work hours drop below 80/month, participant must notify DHS within 10 days.
notes:
Rhode Island DHS explicitly states ABAWDs must 'work and/or volunteer at least 80 hours per month'; volunteer hours count and nonprofits sign a participant log (or complete the ABAWD-3 Combined Activity Reporting Form) with no mandatory E&T enrollment, EDP, county assignment, or MOU pre-step found. Confidence was medium because the ABAWD-3 form text itself is not publicly readable in extractable form and policy guidance is sparse on procedural details — no evidence of a blocking prestep was found but the form's full requirements could not be confirmed from public sources.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial review of the Rhode Island SNAP E&T State Plan FY26 (approved 08/27/2025, https://dhs.ri.gov/media/9541/download?language=en), a primary 86-page policy document. Key findings confirming Tier 1 and justifying high confidence: (1) RI operates a 100% voluntary E&T program — 0 mandatory E&T participants, 100% of all 16,640 work registrants are exempted from mandatory E&T under the 'Voluntary program' category (State Plan pp. 8-9). SNAP E&T enrollment is entirely separate from and independent of meeting the general ABAWD 80-hour volunteer requirement. (2) No workfare component is offered (State Plan p. 37 components checklist, Workfare unchecked). This eliminates any risk that nonprofits hosting volunteers need an MOU or contract with the state. (3) The referral process is explicitly voluntary: 'Participation is voluntary' (State Plan pp. 15-16); ETs inform clients of options and clients choose whether to enroll in E&T. Volunteering 80 hours/month at any nonprofit satisfies the ABAWD requirement without any E&T enrollment. (4) The risnapet.org 'list of possible volunteer opportunities' (United Way, ServeRI, faith-based orgs, libraries, animal rescues) is explicitly a suggestion list, not a pre-approved site registry. (5) No approved volunteer site list, no MOU requirement for hosting organizations, and no case manager assignment step found anywhere in the 86-page state plan. Additional searches conducted: RI SNAP E&T State Plan FY2026, RI SNAP ABAWD work registration intake 2025/2026, RI SNAP ABAWD approved volunteer site list nonprofit form, RI SNAP ABAWD legal aid work requirement 2025/2026. Remote stance for general volunteer activities remains unknown (State Plan shows E&T offers virtual/in-person options but this does not address whether remote volunteer hours count toward the general ABAWD 80-hour requirement). Confidence elevated to high: the FY26 State Plan provides direct policy-level confirmation that the general volunteer lane has no state-imposed prestep, corroborated by RI state regulation (218 RICR 20-00-1.11) and RI DHS ABAWD public page.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Five new dimension groups added. (1) Hours formula: 80 hours/month confirmed federal_80 — multiple RI DHS documents use the exact phrase '80 hours a month'; no workfare formula (RI FY26 State Plan explicitly does not offer a workfare component). (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: policy is silent — the ABAWD-3 form (Rev. 12.8.25, media/11256) asks only for activity location and supervisor signature; no RI document restricts certifying orgs to in-state entities; federal default allows any qualifying nonprofit. (3) Geographic waivers: as of July 1, 2024, reduced from 23 towns to 5 (Central Falls, Charlestown, New Shoreham/Block Island, Providence, Woonsocket) through June 30, 2025 per towns-change letter (media 2024-06); FY26 State Plan (Aug 2025) projects 1,500 of 3,000 ABAWDs still in waived areas, implying some waiver extended into FY26, but exact current list and end date not confirmed in public sources. OBBBA eliminated broad geographic waivers nationally but pre-OBBBA waivers already approved may run to their end dates. (4) Submission: ABAWD-3 form instructs submission at application/interim/recertification via online portal (healthyrhode.ri.gov), HealthyRhode mobile app, mail to PO Box 8709 Cranston RI, or in-person drop box/office. Reporting cadence is at_recertification. 10-day report rule if hours drop. (5) Form fields: ABAWD-3 (extracted via pypdf) has Section 1 (Name, Address, Phone, Case #), Section 2 table (Activity Type, Location, Total Hours/Month, Supervisor Signature per row — up to 7 rows), Section 3 (participant signature + date). Maps to: recipient_name, recipient_address, recipient_case_number, activity_description, hours_total, supervisor_name (implicit from supervisor signature), org_signature, period_start_date, period_end_date. No org_name, org_address, org_EIN, or org_phone fields on the form itself — supervisor signature line is the org attestation. No notarization required.
